scope: Commercial NDAs, MSAs, SaaS Agreements, Vendor Contracts, Employment/Contractor Agreements, Consumer Terms, Creator/Marketplace Terms, Event/Service Agreements, Privacy-Adjacent Policies, Contract-to-Cash correctness, Commercial Leases, Loans/Promissory Notes/Guaranties, Employment Offer & Severance Agreements, Franchise/Distribution Agreements, M&A/Partnership & Operating Agreements, Settlement Agreements, and Construction Contracts
changes: Added 8 high-signal rules (attorneys fees, liability cap carveouts, assignment restrictions, etc.)
Implemented clause-level anchoring and false-positive suppression

v2.0: Added 15 rules for SaaS, vendor, and procurement coverage (unilateral modification, consequential damages, termination for convenience, data portability, asymmetric liability, mandatory arbitration, warranty disclaimers, breach notification, insurance, force majeure, SLA, MFN, compliance, escrow, subcontracting)

v2.1: Added 12 rules for broad ICP coverage: AI/ML model training on customer data, unilateral price escalation, personal data processing without protections, data portability rights, data deletion obligations, cross-border data transfers, renewal pricing increases, minimum purchase commitments, benchmarking restrictions, permitted use restrictions, export controls, payment terms

v3.0: Added 12 broader-audience rules for consumer, creator, freelancer, worker, event, and marketplace terms: stored-card charges, user content licenses, payout deductions, worker classification, refunds, cancellation fees, account suspension, personal data sharing, non-disparagement, media releases, electronic notices, and marketing communications consent

v4.0: Reliability and product-workflow overhaul for Agree.com — evidence offsets now anchor to verbatim source-text slices (no drift from whitespace normalization); proximity-rule evidence spans cover the full anchor+risk-phrase clause instead of a bare trigger word; one-way/unilateral rules now classify party direction (obligor/beneficiary/applies_to/mutuality_status) and no longer flag fully mutual clauses as one-sided; absence-claiming rules (no SLA, no insurance, no breach notification, etc.) split into PresenceRisk vs RequiredSection, with document-level topic/protective-language checks distinguishing 'adverse language detected' from 'expected protection not found' from 'unable to determine'; added a signature_readiness workflow decision layer (ready_to_send / commercial_review_recommended / legal_review_required / blocked_by_policy), additive to and never replacing severity; added 16 contract-to-cash rules (payment/invoice configuration, pricing ambiguity, signature/execution defects, termination-to-billing consequences) plus structured payment_terms extraction (due_days, currency, billing_frequency, invoice_trigger) for direct comparison against an invoice configuration

v5.0: Independent-audit hardening pass. Ingestion: normalize_contract_text() unescapes literal \n sequences before chunking, and _chunk_text() prefers real clause/section boundaries over blind fixed-size slicing. Evidence: bounded previously-unbounded DOTALL proximity spans (H_IP_01, L_LATEFEE_01, and others) so matches can no longer bridge unrelated clauses; fixed M_SLA_01's protective-pattern regex, which structurally could never match a percentage containing a decimal point; fixed a [A-Z0-9] character class that matched any letter case-insensitively, defeating exhibit-identifier checks. Rule-level fixes: H_ATTFEE_01 no longer fires on indemnification defense-cost language; M_ACCOUNT_SUSPEND_01 no longer fires on ordinary termination language; H_DATA_PRIVACY_01 converted from PresenceRisk to RequiredSection so it stops contradicting its own cited evidence; H_TERM_CONVENIENCE_01/M_TERM_NOTICE_01 windows narrowed so they can no longer blend two distinct sub-clauses into one contradictory finding. New party_resolver.py module resolves a contract's OWN defined party names (not just generic role words) to vendor/customer roles, fixing party-direction misclassification when a contract's defined term (e.g. 'Company') happens to be the customer rather than the generic-word-implied vendor. New perspective field on every one-sided/directional finding states favorable/unfavorable relative to the reviewing party, naming the actual contract parties, and downgrades severity when an asymmetry is favorable rather than reporting it identically to an adverse one-sided clause. Added 12 new rules covering previously-undetected material risks: asymmetric liability cap (party-name-aware, not just generic role words), liability cap missing carve-outs, narrow indemnification scope, missing DPA/BAA execution requirements, missing subprocessor terms, missing customer audit rights, missing deletion certification, SLA remedy-exclusivity/chronic-failure gaps, missing insurance minimums, unallocated regulatory responsibility, and conditional/no-default data return. Added a deterministic contradiction-detection pass that reconciles any finding whose title and rationale disagree on directionality. Added root-cause grouping (ROOT_CAUSE_GROUPS) that collapses findings sharing one underlying cause (e.g. a missing SOW/Schedule attachment manifesting as several separately-firing gaps) into one parent finding. Added a CRITICAL severity tier above HIGH for unbounded/asymmetric financial exposure and personal/privacy/security/regulatory risk; raised breach-notification absence from Medium to High.

v6.0: Small/midsize law firm broad-practice expansion — 26 new rules extending coverage beyond SaaS/vendor contracts into the document types a generalist firm reviews most often. Commercial leases: personal guaranty of lease obligations, assignment/sublet consent lacking a reasonableness standard, holdover rent penalties, landlord relocation rights, uncapped CAM charges, uncapped rent escalation. Loans/guaranties: confession-of-judgment/cognovit clauses (CRITICAL), guaranties waiving standard suretyship defenses (CRITICAL), cross-default provisions, prepayment penalties, lender-discretion interest rates. Employment: language undermining at-will status, invention assignments lacking the statutory own-time carve-out, severance releases lacking statutory-rights carve-outs, employee no-solicit/no-hire covenants. Franchise: termination rights lacking a cure period, missing exclusive/protected territory. M&A/partnership: indemnification for reps and warranties lacking a basket/threshold, earn-outs subject to buyer discretion, missing deadlock-resolution mechanisms for equally-held owners, capital-call dilution penalties. Settlement: overbroad releases sweeping in unknown/unanticipated claims, disproportionate liquidated damages. Construction: pay-if-paid clauses, lien waivers required before payment, retainage with no defined release trigger.
